rationale: shellQuoteTarget (single-quote wrap, embedded ' -> '\''), is correct POSIX quoting and closes finding 1 -- verified by reading + the shipped test. escapeConfigValue is correctly applied to every DIRECT target-render call site (missing-ref line, live-tip line, sanitizeNonBeadDecision leg 5). But it is NOT applied where facts.GitErr/f.GitErr is interpolated via %v (panel.go's `case facts.GitErr != nil && nonBead:` and sanitizeNonBeadDecision leg 5b), and gitutil.RevParseRef's non-exit-1 error branch embeds the RAW ref via `fmt.Errorf("rev-parse %s: %w", ref, err)`. I built the real mindspec binary, hand-wrote a panel.json with target containing a JSON \u0000-escaped NUL byte (the exact repo-write-attacker threat model these comments describe) plus an ESC/ANSI sequence and a real newline, and ran `panel tally` against it: exec.Command's NUL-in-argv produces a non-*exec.ExitError, which is NOT IsRefNotFound, so it routes to facts.GitErr (not MissingRef) and BYPASSES the fix. stderr showed the CORRECTLY escaped displayTarget once, then a SECOND, raw, unescaped occurrence of the same hostile target -- literal NUL + ESC[31m + an actual embedded newline byte -- inside the wrapped git-error text. That is exactly the forged-line/control-byte class the fix claims to close. The new HostileTarget test only exercises ESC+newline via the MissingRef (clean exit-1) branch, which doesn't touch this leak, so it passed without covering it. R2 (ms-panel.js) unchanged/sound -- verified byte-identical to round-1 files.
concrete_changes_required: Escape facts.GitErr/f.GitErr before interpolating it via %v anywhere a non-bead target could be embedded in the wrapped error text: renderPanelVerify's `case facts.GitErr != nil && nonBead:` branch (cmd/mindspec/panel.go, ~line 521) and sanitizeNonBeadDecision's leg (5b) transient-error message (~line 826), which currently only string-replaces the 'branch bead/' prefix and leaves gate.go's original `(transient git error: %v)` suffix raw.

Apply escapeConfigValue (or equivalent) to the git error's rendered text at that boundary -- e.g. escapeConfigValue(facts.GitErr.Error()) -- rather than only to the already-safe displayTarget copy used elsewhere in the same message.

Add a pin test that drives the actual GitErr/transient-error branch with a hostile target (a NUL-byte target that fails exec.Command construction, or a stubbed revParseForPanelFn returning a non-ErrRefNotFound error wrapping a hostile string) and asserts no raw control byte/newline reaches combined stdout+stderr -- the current HostileTarget test only covers the MissingRef branch.
findings: shellQuoteTarget is correct: single-quote wrap with '\''-escaping for embedded quotes makes a copied `--target '<x>'`/`--gate '<x>'` argv-safe against ;, $, backticks, and spaces; no break-out found.

escapeConfigValue is a correct safe-set-vs-strconv.Quote escaper and IS applied to every direct target-render path (missing-ref, reviewed_head_sha live-tip, sanitizeNonBeadDecision leg 5) as well as to target/gate before shellQuoteTarget in the recovery command.

RESIDUAL LEAK (empirically confirmed against the real binary): a target with an embedded NUL byte (valid via JSON \u0000 in a hand-edited panel.json) causes exec.Command's argv construction to fail with a non-*exec.ExitError, which gitutil.RevParseRef wraps as `fmt.Errorf("rev-parse %s: %w", ref, err)` WITHOUT the ErrRefNotFound sentinel, so internal/panel/gate.go routes it to f.GitErr rather than f.MissingRef.

In that GitErr branch, panel.go's `%v` on facts.GitErr re-embeds the SAME raw target a second time in the same output line, unescaped -- confirmed via od -c on captured stdout/stderr: a target of NUL+ESC+"[31m"+newline+"recovery: FORGED_LINE..." produced a literal raw NUL byte, a literal raw ESC/ANSI sequence, and a literal raw embedded newline byte in stderr, splitting the advisory into an extra physical line.

The same untouched raw-%v pattern exists in sanitizeNonBeadDecision's leg (5b) transient-error message (gate.go's shared template), where only the 'could not verify branch bead/' prefix is string-replaced; the trailing '(transient git error: %v)' text is passed through unmodified.

TestPanelTally_NonBeadHostileTargetEscapedAndQuoted passes but does not pin this: its control-byte subtest (ESC+newline, no NUL) resolves via a clean exit-1 rev-parse failure (MissingRef branch), which was already safe before this fix and never exercises facts.GitErr.

R2 (ms-panel.js) unchanged from round 1: plugins/mindspec/workflows/ms-panel.js and .claude/workflows/ms-panel.js remain byte-identical (diff empty); validateTarget's create-time control-byte/whitespace/git-ref-format/shell-safe checks still hold for anything written through the CLI/workflow, consistent with the stated hand-edited-panel.json threat model for this residual gap.
